content: EF Core namespace rename and migration compatibility: The __EFMigrationsHistory table tracks migrations by MigrationId (timestamp + name) and ProductVersion — NOT by namespace. Renaming namespaces in migration files does NOT break the history table. You must update the C# namespace in migration class files and the Designer.cs files, but the DB history is unaffected. The DbContext class full name IS used as the ContextKey in EF6, but EF Core uses a different mechanism — migrations are matched by assembly name in the snapshot. Safest path: rename namespaces in all migration .cs files, update the model snapshot namespace, rebuild — no DB surgery needed for EF Core (unlike EF6).
